content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 25, 1990


Marian Staton
Treasurer
Friends of Lloyd Mashore
4255 Clayton Road
Concord, CA  94521

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-042

Dear Ms. Staton:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice provided to you on behalf of Lloyd Mashore
regarding the propriety of using campaign funds to pay for legal
fees under the new personal use provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").1/

This letter confirms that your letter dated January 8, 1990
accurately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you on
that date.  As I stated in our telephone conversation, Section
85802.5 of the Act provides:

(a)  Expenditures of campaign funds for
attorney's fees and other costs in connection with
administrative, civil, or criminal litigation are
not directly related to a political, legislative,
or governmental purpose except where the litigation
arises directly out of a candidate's or elected
officer's activities, duties, or status as a
candidate or elected officer, including, but not
limited to, an action to enjoin defamation, defense
of an action to enjoin defamation, defense of an
action brought for a violation of state or local
campaign, disclosure, or election laws, and an
action arising from an election contest or recount.

Thus, where the litigation arises directly out of a
candidate's status as a candidate, such as where his

_______________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. A-90-042
Page 2


qualifications as a candidate as specified in the elections code
are challenged, the attorney fees associated with the litigation
are directly related to a political purpose. Therefore, use of
campaign funds to pay the attorney fees and other costs associated
with the litigation is permitted under the Act.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact this office at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
KED:JW:plh

sections.question_synthetic: Whether campaign funds may be used to pay attorney's fees and litigation costs when a candidate's qualifications or status as a candidate are challenged under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign funds may be used to pay attorney's fees and other litigation costs when the litigation arises directly out of a candidate's status as a candidate, such as challenges to the candidate's qualifications specified in the Elections Code, as permitted under Government Code section 85802.5.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice; no explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers are present, but the legal question and answer are clearly articulated in the body. The document cites Government Code section 85802.5 as the governing statute. Minor OCR artifacts present (e.g., 'J' in date, spacing irregularities).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether campaign funds may be used to pay attorney's fees and litigation costs when a candidate's qualifications or status as a candidate are challenged under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Campaign funds may be used to pay attorney's fees and other litigation costs when the litigation arises directly out of a candidate's status as a candidate, such as challenges to the candidate's qualifications specified in the Elections Code, as permitted under Government Code section 85802.5.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel confirms telephone advice to the treasurer of Friends of Lloyd Mashore regarding the permissibility of using campaign funds to pay legal fees for litigation arising from challenges to the candidate's qualifications or status under the Political Reform Act's personal use provisions.
